title: Domain Adaptation: Why Your Model Breaks When the Background Changes
description: Car classifiers that fail in garages, X-ray models that break across hospitals, and how MMD forces neural networks to learn features that transfer across domains.

title: Invariance and Equivariance: Teaching Neural Networks About Symmetry
description: Why CNNs break when you rotate the input, how the Reynolds operator builds symmetry into weight space, and a 1,800-parameter network that crushed a 454,000-parameter CNN.

title: Sharp vs. Flat Minima: Why Some Models Generalize and Others Just Memorize
description: Neural networks can memorize random labels. So why do they generalize on real data? The answer is in the geometry of the loss surface.

title: Disentangled Representations: Why VAEs Can't Disentangle (and What Actually Works)
description: Locatello et al. proved unsupervised disentanglement is impossible. Here's why rotational symmetry breaks identifiability, and how sparsity fixes it.

title: Why Your Model Works in the Notebook but Breaks in Production
description: Covariate shift, concept drift, retraining strategies, and the monitoring that keeps ML systems alive after deployment.

title: Recommendation Systems: From Matrix Factorization to Two-Tower Models
description: Collaborative filtering, content-based methods, two-tower retrieval, GNN-based recs, and the popularity bias problem that breaks most systems.

title: Airflow and DAGs: The Orchestration Tool Every Data Team Ends Up Using
description: What Airflow is, how DAGs work, building your first ML pipeline, error handling, and when to use something else instead.

title: Spark, Hadoop, and PySpark: When You Actually Need Distributed Computing
description: Hadoop's legacy, why Spark replaced it, PySpark for ML feature engineering, and the honest threshold for when single-machine tools stop working.

title: Deploying ML Models with SageMaker: Endpoints, Scaling, and When Not to Bother
description: Real-time and serverless endpoints, auto-scaling, A/B testing with production variants, cost optimization, and when a FastAPI server is the better choice.

title: Getting Started with Claude Code: Everything I Wish I Knew on Day One
description: A beginner's guide to Claude Code covering CLAUDE.md, context management, key commands, and the feedback loop that makes it actually useful.

title: Claude Code Beyond the Basics: Agents, Context Engineering, and Production Workflows
description: An intermediate guide to Claude Code covering context management, CLAUDE.md patterns, subagents, custom commands, hooks, MCP, skills, and CI integration.

title: Context Poisoning: How Attackers Manipulate LLM Context Windows
description: Prompt injection, data poisoning in RAG systems, and adversarial inputs that hijack LLM behavior. Real attack vectors and practical defenses for production systems.

title: Building a RAG System from Scratch
description: A step-by-step guide to building retrieval-augmented generation with Python. Embeddings, vector search, and generation, no frameworks required.

title: What is Fine-Tuning, and Why Does Every AI Company Do It?
description: RLHF, LoRA, full fine-tuning explained without the jargon. How companies take a base model and turn it into something actually useful.

title: Fitting Big Models on Small GPUs
description: Quantization techniques (GPTQ, GGUF, AWQ), distillation as a size reduction strategy, and practical tradeoffs for running 70B models on consumer hardware.

title: Model Distillation: The Technique, The Controversy, and How To Do It
description: What model distillation is, how DeepSeek allegedly used it on frontier model outputs, and a practical guide with lessons from our reasoning distillation paper.

title: Automated Rule Generation for Fraud Detection Using Deep Learning and Explainability
description: How to automatically extract interpretable, auditable fraud detection rules from LSTM models using SHAP, Anchors, knowledge distillation, and temporal pattern mining.

title: UrbanTherm: GPU-Accelerated Surface Energy Balance for Urban Microclimate Modeling
description: How we extended SOLWEIG with a full surface energy balance solver, Newton-Raphson surface temperatures, multi-layer conduction, canopy transpiration, and spatial air temperature, all running on GPU at 1m resolution.

title: Industry-Level RAG Practices: A Comprehensive Reference
description: A detailed guide covering the full RAG pipeline: chunking, embeddings, vector databases, retrieval strategies, reranking, advanced architectures (CRAG, Self-RAG, Graph RAG, RAPTOR), evaluation, and production deployment.
